pattern: Every bit set in any element contributes to exactly half of all subsets, so the answer is the bitwise OR of all elements multiplied by two to the power of n minus one.
hints: The array is tiny, so enumerating every subset and XORing its elements is entirely feasible.
Look at a single bit position: across all subsets, that bit ends up set in exactly half of them whenever at least one element has it.
Compute the OR of all elements, then multiply by 2^(n-1); equivalently, recurse over subsets accumulating each subset's XOR total.

edge_inputs.description: max length all equal to max value
duplicates do not cancel out across subsets

stress.description: random arrays at or near the maximum length over the full value range
